parsed_review.summary: CP00-182 (Risk B, 40 units, RP05.P04.M02.S08-RP05.P04.M04.S17) is a descriptor-only Matter Core UI interaction workflow pack. Independent read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob only) confirms the pack supports its eventual production_ready claim and is safe to close. Key verifications: (1) The UI workflow path (executeMatterCoreUiWorkflow / createMatterCoreUiWorkflowFixture in packages/matter/src/service.js) is pure and composes CP181 API + CP178 service descriptors; every result is frozen via freezeUiWorkflowResult with writes_product_state, renders_live_dom, executes_api_handler, dispatches_review_route, dispatches_approval_route, issues_network_request, leaks_unauthorized_counts, and implements_loop_engine all false. No filesystem/network/DB I/O exists in the path, so the no-write/no-real-data attestation matches actual behavior; all fixtures use synthetic tenant/matter IDs. (2) Claude is held to read_only_independent_reviewer and explicitly not final approver in registry.js, contracts/matter-core-contract.json (claude_is_final_approval=false), and validators.js (promotes_claude_to_final_approval guard); enterprise trust is reserved for human approval and not claimed from local validation. (3) Hermes patterns appear only as the H05 gate label and the pure createMatterCoreCp182HermesEvidencePacket descriptor function — no runtime engine is embedded in the product, consistent with hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false. (4) The deliverable distribution (ui 18 / implementation 10 / claude_review 3 / security_audit 4 / fixture 2 / test 2 / hermes_evidence 1 = 40) and micro-phase split (M02 1 / M03 22 / M04 17) were independently recounted from the 40 baseline units and match; validateMatterCoreCp182Coverage computes these from real included_units, not hardcoded. (5) Tests exercise denied/review_required/approval_required/ready states, ref-only permission badge and audit hint, customer-safe error copy, keyboard focus order, and unauthorized-count non-leak. (6) Review-hardening preflight is healthy: readiness auth passed (exit 0, 1434-byte stdout, valid JSON), no auth failure / 0-byte / malformed / fenced-prose / tool-shaped review evidence is present, and no invalid receipts. The pack-level production_ready flag is correctly still false/pending across manifest, command-evidence, construction-inspection, and adjudication — appropriately gated behind this review plus adjudication, inspection, and final validation. Only two minor P3 informational items were found (validation completeness and a generic safe-error-copy fallback); neither affects the descriptor-only boundary, no-write guarantees, or the authority model, and neither blocks pack or goal closeout. As read-only reviewer I do not grant final approval; human sign-off remains required for any enterprise trust claim.

findings.title: CP182 UI-workflow validator does not assert required_interactions or the full display-guard set
findings.details: validateMatterCoreCp182UiWorkflow checks all six required_states, three key safe-error codes, and only five of the ten required_display_guards, and does not assert membership of the five required_interactions (open_descriptor_preview, open_review_queue_descriptor, open_approval_route_descriptor, refresh_descriptor_without_mutation, show_safe_error_summary). The contract, registry requirements, and the test suite (via executeMatterCoreUiWorkflow) do cover these, so current behavior is correct; this is a drift-protection completeness gap only — a future edit that drops an interaction or one of the unasserted guards from MATTER_CORE_CP182_UI_WORKFLOW_REQUIREMENTS would not be caught by this validator.
findings.recommendation: Extend validateMatterCoreCp182UiWorkflow to assert all required_interactions and all ten required_display_guards are present, mirroring the existing required_states loop, to harden against silent requirement drift. Non-blocking for CP00-182.
findings.post_review_disposition: open_non_blocking_informational — recommend addressing in a future RP05 UI hardening pass; does not gate CP00-182 pack or goal closeout

findings.title: Safe-error copy falls back to generic MATTER_CORE_BLOCKED text for blocked codes outside the curated map
findings.details: safeErrorCopyFor resolves customer_safe_error_codes against MATTER_CORE_CP182_UI_WORKFLOW_REQUIREMENTS.safe_error_copy and falls back to the generic MATTER_CORE_BLOCKED copy for codes not in that map (e.g., MATTER_CORE_TENANT_SCOPE_MISSING, MATTER_CORE_IDEMPOTENCY_REQUIRED, MATTER_CORE_PERMISSION_ENVELOPE_REQUIRED, MATTER_CORE_INVALID_STATUS, MATTER_CORE_SOURCE_LINK_REQUIRED). These are reachable (e.g., a tenant_scope_missing block). The fallback is customer-safe and leaks no internal detail, so this is purely a copy-specificity/UX completeness note, not a data-exposure or boundary issue.
findings.recommendation: Either add tailored safe copy entries for the remaining blocked codes the service boundary can emit, or document the generic MATTER_CORE_BLOCKED fallback as the intentional default. Non-blocking for CP00-182.
findings.post_review_disposition: open_non_blocking_informational — copy refinement only; customer-safe today and does not gate CP00-182 closeout
